title: Amber Fields
description: A farming village on the southwestern lowlands, named for the color of its grain at harvest. Far from any political center, it has survived wars, famines, and the Post-Collapse by being too small to matter to armies and too productive to destroy. Solan Vorne was born here.

title: Ashveil
description: The ruins of an ancient city, destroyed in an event known only as The Burning. The stones are still black with soot after two centuries. No one settles here — locals say the ash shifts on windless nights. Scholars believe it was once larger than Vareth.

title: Deepwater Docks
description: A purpose-built harbor town on the southeastern coast, carved into a natural deep-water bay that can berth ships too large for Port Maren. The town has no civic identity beyond its function — it exists to load and unload cargo. Half the population works the docks; the other half exists to feed and shelter the first half.

title: Drenford
description: A mid-sized town where the West River is narrow enough to ford. Famous for its bridge market — travelers between north and south stop here to trade. Nominally part of the Free Coast but too small to matter in politics.

title: Eastern Hold
description: The easternmost outpost of any known political entity — a bleak fortification on the edge of the unmapped eastern plains. For most of its history it served as a relay station for trade caravans and a deterrent against eastern raids. In the Post-Collapse, the Pale King made it his seat of power.

title: The Grey Keep
description: A solitary fortress on the northwestern cliffs, built before recorded history. House Voss uses it as a military outpost and prison. The stones are a pale grey that turns almost white in fog.

title: Ironwood Hall
description: A great hall built inside and around a grove of ironwood trees so old their trunks have grown through the walls. Neutral ground by tradition — no faction has ever garrisoned it, no army has marched through it, and no record exists of its original construction. The First Council of Kings was held here.

title: Morngate
description: The greatest trading city of the eastern reaches, built on a high ridge overlooking the plains below. Its white towers are visible from a day's ride in any direction. The city changed hands three times during the Long Wars, and each new ruler found the merchant guilds more powerful than the armies that claimed it.

title: Port Maren
description: The largest city on the southern coast. A chaotic, vibrant port where three languages are spoken daily. The Free Coast alliance maintains its loose capital here. Heavily defended against sea raiders.

title: Saltmere
description: A small fishing and salt-harvesting village at the mouth of a shallow coastal inlet. The salt pans here produce a distinctive grey salt prized by kitchens across the Known Lands. Kessa of Saltmere was born here and named after it, which she considers the most useful inheritance her family ever gave her.

title: Solmark
description: A prosperous trading city on the eastern shore of the Veiled Sea. Governed by a council of merchant houses — no single ruler. Ships from the southern coasts bring silk, spice, and contraband. The Compact was born here.

title: Stonecrown
description: A fortress carved directly into a coastal promontory, its battlements rising above the western cliffs like a crown of dark stone. Built to guard the sea lanes against raiders from beyond the western horizon. The garrison has never exceeded three hundred, but the position has held against forces ten times that size.

title: Thornwood Watch
description: A lone watchtower on the edge of the Thornwood forest, staffed by Northern Reach scouts. The forest below it is ancient and largely unmapped. The Watch has been continuously occupied since Year 60.

title: Vale of Whispers
description: A hidden valley in the northern highlands where the wind through the standing stones sounds like conversation. No settlement exists here — only the stones, a cold stream, and a single ancient tree of a species no scholar has ever named. Lirien of the Vale is the only permanent resident anyone has ever recorded.

title: Vareth
description: The largest fortified city in the Northern Reach, built where the Var River meets the foothills of the Ashspine. Ruled by House Voss for six generations. Its iron gates have never been breached in recorded history. The city smells of coal smoke and river mist.

title: Veiled Shore
description: A small fishing village on the southern shore of the Veiled Sea. The lake is unnaturally still here — no waves, no birds over the water. Locals harvest a salt-resistant reed that no one has found elsewhere. Some say the sea has a bottom no one has reached.
